pattern: Once you notice the score at a split only needs a running prefix sum and a running suffix minimum, both can be swept in a single pass without recomputing either from scratch at every index.
hints: You need two numbers at every candidate split point: the sum of everything to the left (inclusive) and the smallest value anywhere to the right. Neither has to be recomputed from scratch each time.
Walk left to right maintaining a running prefix sum. Separately, precompute for every index the minimum of all elements strictly after it, by walking once from the right end.

With both arrays available in O(1) lookup per index, just take the max of prefixSum[i] - suffixMin[i] over every valid split i from 0 to n-2, tracking a running best as you go instead of storing both full arrays if you want O(1) extra space.

edge_inputs.description: increasing values, best split is not at the end
single huge negative at the very end drags suffix min down for every split
single huge negative at the very start only affects the split that includes it in the prefix
extreme magnitude values at both ends

stress.description: random array with a wide value range, exercising every split via the O(n) sweep (kept modest so the O(n^2) oracle finishes quickly)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000000000, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
